output: Executed batch 5 task T9. `execute_share_to_social()` now parses `reply_to_tweet` as either a raw tweet ID or a `status/<digits>` URL, rejects malformed reply targets, forwards the parsed ID into `finalize_sharing()`, no longer blocks text-only messages, and returns the actual `tweet_url`/`tweet_id`/`already_shared` fields from the sharer result. Syntax verification passed via `python -m py_compile`, and the batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/add-tweet-thread-reply-20260408-0423/execution_batch_5.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Updated `execute_share_to_social()` to parse `reply_to_tweet` as either a raw numeric ID or a tweet URL containing `status/<digits>`, reject malformed values with a clear error, and pass the parsed ID into `sharer.finalize_sharing(..., in_reply_to_tweet_id=...)`. Removed the executor’s old no-attachments rejection so text-only messages can flow through, and rebuilt the tool response from the returned result dict to include `tweet_url`, `tweet_id`, `already_shared`, plus the expected success/failure message variants. Verified by compiling `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: `execute_share_to_social()` now parses both raw numeric IDs and `status/<digits>` URLs into the same reply tweet ID, rejects malformed inputs, forwards the parsed ID into `finalize_sharing()`, and returns `tweet_url`, `tweet_id`, and `already_shared` in the tool response.
